channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: 16 fascinating breakthroughs of 2024 that weren’t about AI
item.description: All the excitement around artificial intelligence can make it hard to notice all the other exciting progress lately that’s been the result of human intelligence. Of course, it’s getting harder to say what’s not got some bit of AI or deep learning in it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Pasternack, Fast Company

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Inside Pinterest’s quest to restore internet optimism
item.description: Most social media platforms have devolved into a network of bottom-feeders and rage-baiters. Yet somehow, Pinterest has kept its joy. According to Sprout Social’s 2024 content strategy report, more than half of social users think of Pinterest as “more positive” than other platforms.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Why Apple TV+ is offering a free weekend of binge-watching
item.description: Apple TV+ is hoping people will make a dent in the Strategic Popcorn Reserve by bingeing its streaming TV and movies for free this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Mark Kennedy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Turo rentals emerge as common thread in Las Vegas Cybertruck and New Orleans deadly incidents
item.description: Turo faces scrutiny after tragic incidents involving rented vehicles, raising questions about security measures and regulatory frameworks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PS3 Emulation on PS5: Implicit Conversions "Dreaming About" Native Support
item.description: Implicit Conversions' roadmap reveals plans for PS3 emulation, potentially for the PlayStation Classics collection on PS5.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: For Apple IT admins, the new year means it’s a good time to upskill
item.description: Continuing education has always been important for IT professionals, but the need to keep your skill set up to date is now more important than ever.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Working from the office means a pay cut
item.description: In recent months, companies have been forcing workers back into the office with increasing frequency. Annoyingly, many of these are big tech businesses that can most easily enable their workers to work remotely. This trend, likely to continue in 2025, costs workers real money.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Top 9 generative AI use cases for business
item.description: The launch of ChatGPT in November 2022 set off a generative AI gold rush, with companies scrambling to adopt the technology and demonstrate innovation.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The cloud architecture renaissance of 2025
item.description: The perfect storm is coming that will force enterprises to rethink their cloud strategy. Cloud architecture will take center stage during 2025. This isn’t just another hype cycle. First, we need to talk about the elephant in the room: generative AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Joel Kaplan to replace Nick Clegg as Meta global policy leader
item.description: Meta will switch up its global policy team in the new year and has appointed former George W. Bush administration official Joel Kaplan to replace Nick Clegg as the company's chief global affairs officer.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: The US government must keep pace with serving an AI-empowered citizenry
item.description: Our government needs to maintain trust through competence, and that competence will increasingly be about powering government services with AI. The post The US government must keep pace with serving an AI-empowered citizenry first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: What to do if your manager keeps watching your Instagram Stories
item.description: There are certain social media rules we can all agree on: Ghosting a conversation is impolite, and replying “k” to a text is the equivalent of a backhand slap (violent, wrong, and rude).
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: Rhode Island’s state health benefits website hacked, user data exposed
item.description: Cybercriminals who hacked Rhode Island’s system for health and benefits programs have released files to a site on the dark web, a scenario the state has been preparing for, Gov. Daniel McKee said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: The biggest Wi-Fi mistake you didn’t know you were making
item.description: There are many untapped safeguards and tips you can learn to take your Wi-Fi knowledge to the next level, technology expert Kim Komando shares her tricks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rogue Two
item.description: Don Parente, the vice president of sales and solution architecture for MetTel Federal, provides recommendations for agencies to better manage space IT. The post Rogue Two — Your IT sprawl is about to expand to space first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Revealed: The 10 most popular and worst passwords of 2024
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson cites a new report that says many people still use weak passwords like "123456," risking their accounts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Venezuela's top court issues a $10 million fine for TikTok over allegedly deadly video challenges
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuela’s Supreme Court on Monday issued a $10 million fine against TikTok for “not implementing measures” to prevent viral video challenges that have allegedly led to the deaths of three Venezuelan children recently.
item.dc:creator: JUAN PABLO ARRAEZ and MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The long-term impacts of AI on networking
item.description: AI is either going to spawn a golden age of profits and benefits or kill us all off, according to popular viewpoints on the subject. The latter view has been at least somewhat discredited; if you yell “AI” in a crowded theater these days, hardly anyone runs for the exits.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Windows Defender Security Center scam: How to protect your computer from fake pop-ups
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says a tech support scam used a fake Windows Defender pop-up, tricking the victim to call and download software.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Android 15's new Private Space feature puts an end to app snooping
item.description: Android 15's latest feature, Private Space, is like a private vault helping to keep your personal or sensitive apps away from prying eyes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
